channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Takeaways from AP’s report on pardoned Jan. 6 rioters being embraced in Republican politics
item.description: JACKSON, Mich. (AP) — Those who rioted, assaulted police officers or broke into congressional offices during the riot at the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021 are now being received as honored guest speakers at local Republican events around the country.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Guatemalan national indicted for allegedly smuggling teen girl into the US
item.description: A Guatemalan national residing in the U.S. illegally was indicted for allegedly encouraging a teen girl to enter the country unlawfully and making false claims to gain custody of her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oklahoma City bombing survivor was ‘getting ready to die’ after being trapped in 10 feet of rubble
item.description: Thirty years ago on April 19, 1995, a truck bomb detonated outside the Alfred P. Murrah Federal Building, killing 168 people in the deadliest homegrown attack on U.S. soil.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Menendez brothers could get freedom under California law signed by Gavin Newsom: expert
item.description: A California trial lawyer breaks down an analysis of what the judge in the Menendez brothers resentencing hearings is considering when factoring in a potential prison release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court blocks, for now, new deportations under 18th century wartime law
item.description: The Supreme Court on Saturday blocked, for now, the deportations of any Venezuelans held in northern Texas under an 18th century wartime law.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Romania's presidential election redo is a high-stakes test of its democracy following annulled vote
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romania's presidential election redo next month will be a high-stakes test for the European Union member’s democracy after last year’s annulled vote triggered the country’s deepest political crisis since 1989. Romania’s Constitutional Court annulled the election on Dec.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and NICOLAE DUMITRACHE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about the tensions between Iran and the US before their second round of talks
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran and the United States will hold talks Saturday in Rome, their second round of negotiations over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program. The talks follow a first round held in Muscat, Oman, where the two sides spoke face to face.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration makes major cuts to Native American boarding school research projects
item.description: At least $1.6 million in federal funds for projects meant to capture and digitize stories of the systemic abuse of generations of Indigenous children in boarding schools at the hands of the U.S. government have been slashed due to federal funding cuts under President Donald Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former President Bill Clinton returns to Oklahoma City 30 years after the bombing
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Thirty years after the deadliest homegrown attack in U.S. history, former President Bill Clinton will return to Oklahoma City on Saturday to remember the people who were killed and comfort those affected by the bombing.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Questions emerge about how a deputy's stepson became the accused gunman in deadly FSU shooting
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Amid the abandoned chemistry notes and other debris left behind after a deadly shooting at Florida State University are lingering questions about how the stepson of a beloved sheriff’s deputy tasked with school safety at a middle school became the accused gunman.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s first quarter approval rating sits at 45 percent: Poll
item.description: Less than half of U.S. adults say they have confidence in the president to recommend or do the right thing for the economy, according to the Gallup poll.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Judge pauses Trump administration's plans for mass layoffs at Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
item.description: The decision leaves in limbo a bureau created after the Great Recession to safeguard against fraud, abuse and deceptive practices. Trump administration officials argue that it has overstepped its authority and should have a more limited mission.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Deliberations to resume Monday at bribery trial of Nadine Menendez, wife of ex-US Sen. Bob Menendez
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A jury will resume deliberations Monday in the bribery case brought against Nadine Menendez after a prosecutor told a jury that she was a “critical participant” in a five-year fraud with her husband, former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Sen. Cornyn asks Trump for more funds for flood-stricken South Texas
item.description: Republican Sen. John Cornyn, of Texas, sent a letter Friday to President Trump asking for more help for flood-stricken South Texas, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Google reveals California’s favorite Easter candy
item.description: Everyone has their go-to Easter treat—be it chocolate eggs, jellybeans, or gummies—but a new Google Trends study shows which sweet was the favorite in each state. Peeps were found to be Californians’ top Easter candy choice.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: A US citizen was held for pickup by ICE even after proving he was born in the country
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A U.S. citizen was arrested in Florida for allegedly being in the country illegally and held for pickup by immigration authorities even after his mother showed a judge her son’s birth certificate and the judge dismissed charges.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s claims on Kilmar Abrego Garcia largely rely on 1 informant
item.description: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's immigration troubles started in 2019, when he was arrested outside of a Maryland Home Depot.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Kilmar Abrego-Garcia suspected of human trafficking in report obtained by Fox News
item.description: The Homeland Security report states that "official law enforcement investigations" revealed Abrego-Garcia was a member of the notorious gang MS-13.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 food service employees killed in FSU shooting
item.description: A food service worker was identified as one of two people killed when a gunman opened fire on the Florida State University campus Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: FDA hiring contractors to replace terminated safety inspection staffers
item.description: When Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced sweeping job cuts at his department last month, he said that safety inspectors who oversee U.S. foods and drugs wouldn't be impacted.
item.dc:creator: By MATTHEW PERRONE AP Health Writer, KRON4

item.title: Pennsylvania senator asked child 'pornography' question during civics competition
item.description: "I am further appalled and furious that I now have to explain pornography to my fourth grader," the mom said.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, NewsNation

item.title: NASA's Lucy spacecraft is speeding toward another close encounter with an asteroid
item.description: NASA’s Lucy spacecraft will swoop past a small asteroid this weekend as it makes its way to an even bigger prize: the unexplored swarms of asteroids out near Jupiter.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ryan Gosling to star in next 'Star Wars' film, Lucasfilm announces
item.description: The Force was with many Japanese, as well as visitors from abroad, at a “Star Wars” event on Friday where Lucasfilm announced the next installation in the film franchise.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, KRON4

item.title: Trump's claims about Abrego Garcia’s gang ties largely rely on 1 confidential tip
item.description: The Trump administration’s argument that the mistakenly deported Kilmar Abrego Garcia is a member of MS-13 is based largely on a confidential tip he and his family have long disputed.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: Get an Apple discount by recycling your old tech
item.description: Step inside an Apple Store and it’s hard not to notice the shiny new gadgets, but did you know you can also bring in your old tech (even non Apple stuff!) to trade in for a gift card or recycle for free? Right now, you can even nab a 10% Earth Day discount; more details
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Crews restore power to nearly all clients after island-wide blackout hit Puerto Rico
item.description: Power was restored to 98.8% of clients early Friday after an island-wide blackout hit Puerto earlier this week, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: More than 1,000 international students have had visas or legal status revoked
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 1,000 international students have had their visas or legal status revoked in recent weeks, and several have filed lawsuits against the Trump administration, arguing the government denied them due process when it suddenly took away their permission to be in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctor in Germany accused of 15 murders, suspected in 95 more
item.description: A 40-year-old doctor practicing palliative medicine in Germany has been charged with killing 15 of his patients and is suspected in another 95 cases, NBC News reported.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Federal judge in Baltimore temporarily limits DOGE access to Social Security data
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday imposed new restrictions on billionaire Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, limiting its access to Social Security systems that hold personal data on millions of Americans. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FSU lecturer finds broken doors in building after 2-hour shooting lockdown
item.description: Busted doors and debris were what a lecturer at Florida State University found after being locked down for hours during Thursday's mass shooting.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: The Latest: 2 people killed and at least 6 wounded in Florida State shooting
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — A gunman opened fire Thursday at Florida State University, killing two people and wounding at least six others, police said. The 20-year-old gunman is the son of a sheriff’s deputy whose former service weapon was used in the shooting, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare killing suspect Luigi Mangione indicted as prosecutors push for death penalty
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Luigi Mangione was indicted Thursday on a federal murder charge in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, a required step as prosecutors work to make good on the Trump administration’s order to seek the death penalty for what it called a “premeditated, cold-blooded assas
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Train collides with funeral home limo in Tennessee; family of 6 injured
item.description: A funeral limo en route to a funeral — and carrying a family to the funeral — was struck by a train in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, Fox 5

item.title: Trump calls FSU shooting a ‘shame,’ says he’ll always protect Second Amendment
item.description: President Trump on Thursday called a deadly shooting at Florida State University (FSU) “terrible,” but he dismissed the possibility of it spurring any changes to gun laws in the country.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Arson attack probe at Pennsylvania governor's mansion looking into suspect's hatred of Josh Shapiro
item.description: HERSHEY, Pa. (AP) — Authorities investigating why a man set fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s official residence are probing whether the suspect was motivated by the Democrat's Jewish faith or positions on Israel’s war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wrong man, wrong bed, wrong treatment: Patient's death prompts medical board to discipline California doctor
item.description: A California physician has been placed on five years’ probation by the state's medical board for administering a course of treatment to a patient that was intended for the patient in an adjacent hospital bed.
item.dc:creator: Robert Price, KRON4

item.title: Former MLS player Aaron Boupendza dies after falling from building in China, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4
